_meta.description: Known alert patterns with defined solutions. Agent classifies as ACTIONABLE only if matched here.

steps: Perform HTTP health check on the host URL
Attempt web service restart via SSM Run Command (nginx/apache/iis)
Re-run HTTP health check — if recovered: resolved
If service restart fails or website still down: restart EC2 instance (tag-gated: auto-restart=true)
Re-run HTTP health check after EC2 restart
If still down: escalate to human
solutions.resolution_message: Website was unreachable. Service restarted and verified healthy.

steps: Send client communication email using high-memory template
Include current memory utilization value and affected host details
Advise client to review memory-intensive processes
Log incident and monitor for recurrence
If memory stays critical (>95%) for 30 min: escalate to human
solutions.resolution_message: Memory utilization alert communicated to client. Monitoring for resolution.

steps: Attempt local service restart via SSM Run Command: net start AwsReplicationVolumeUpdaterService
Wait 60 seconds and verify service status
If local restart fails: attempt SSM Run Command full service reset
Wait 60 seconds and verify again
If SSM restart fails: restart EC2 instance (tag-gated: auto-restart=true)
Verify service running after EC2 restart
If still failing: escalate to human
zabbix_triggers: Windows: "AwsReplicationVolumeUpdaterService" (AwsReplicationVolumeUpdaterService) is not running (startup type automatic)

steps: Attempt Zabbix agent service restart via SSM Run Command
Linux: sudo systemctl restart zabbix-agent2
Windows: Restart-Service -Name 'Zabbix Agent'
Wait 2 minutes and verify agent connectivity in Zabbix
If agent comes back: resolved
If agent still down: escalate to human for manual investigation
solutions.resolution_message: Zabbix agent service restarted and connectivity restored.
zabbix_triggers: Linux: Zabbix agent is not available (or nodata for 30m)

steps: Send notification to ops team — active checks unavailable on host
Log incident for monitoring
No automated action taken — monitor for recurrence
zabbix_triggers: Linux: Active checks are not available

steps: Send client communication email using high-cpu template for their AWS account
Include current CPU utilization, affected host, and time of alert
Advise client to identify high-CPU processes

solutions.pattern: CPU queue length is too high
steps: Send client communication email using high-cpu template for their AWS account
Include CPU queue length value and affected host details
zabbix_triggers: Windows: CPU queue length is too high (over 3 for 5m)

steps: Send client communication email using disk-space template for their AWS account
Include current disk usage, filesystem, and affected host
Advise client to clean up or request volume expansion
zabbix_triggers: Linux: disk space is critically low

steps: Check if instance belongs to an Auto Scaling Group (tag: aws:autoscaling:groupName exists)
If Auto Scaling instance: ignore — ASG will replace it automatically
If NOT Auto Scaling instance: immediate escalation to human
Create high-priority ticket and send urgent email
Send informational email to client using host-restarted template
No remediation required
zabbix_triggers: Linux: System has just been restarted

steps: Send client communication email using high-load template for their AWS account
Include load average value and affected host
zabbix_triggers: Linux: Load average is too high (per CPU load over 1.5 for 5m)
